seoTitle: How to Sync Microsoft Loop Tasks With Microsoft To Do
metaDescription: Connect a Microsoft Loop task list to Microsoft To Do so assigned items appear in your personal tracker and stay updated in both directions.
intro: Copying tracking notes manually between a shared Loop page and your personal task app wastes time and leads to dropped items. This guide covers connecting a Loop task list to Microsoft To Do so assignments automatically appear in your personal tray and completions sync back to the shared page.

steps.heading: Create a task grid on your Loop page
steps.body: Type the /task list slash command on an empty line to drop a fresh collaborative task grid onto the canvas.

steps.heading: Set task names and due dates
steps.body: Type each core task name and use the connected calendar picker to assign a target due date.

steps.heading: Assign an owner
steps.body: Click the Assigned To column and select a team member to trigger the link into their personal task app.

steps.heading: Activate the background sync
steps.body: Go to the page header, click the Task Apps button, and select Open in Planner to finalize the backend connection to Microsoft To Do.

steps.heading: Verify the sync in To Do
steps.body: Open your personal Microsoft To Do app, check the 'Assigned to me' category, and confirm the task appears there.

steps.heading: Test bidirectional updates
steps.body: Check off the task inside To Do, then return to your Loop page to confirm the status updated automatically.

faq.question: Do I have to assign a task to myself to see it in To Do?
faq.answer: Yes, the Assigned To field is what determines whether a task appears in a specific person's personal To Do tray.

faq.question: What happens if I check off a task in Loop instead of To Do?
faq.answer: The sync is designed to work in both directions, so completing a task in either app should update its status in the other.

faq.question: Why isn't my task syncing to To Do?
faq.answer: This is often caused by a missing assignment, an unrefreshed connection, or a detached component — try reassigning the task or refreshing your account settings.

faq.question: Can multiple people be assigned the same Loop task?
faq.answer: The sync is generally built around single ownership per row, so it's cleaner to split shared work into separate assigned rows.
